commands.review_summary:
The PR hardens Windows node pairing identity, loopback gateway auth classification, node exec-approval RPCs, native approval policy handling, and read-only Control UI rendering for host-native node policies.

PR surface: Source +1127, Tests +2701, Config 0, Other 0. Total +3828 across 64 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and the PR's Windows proof: the affected pairing, auth, and approval paths are clear, although this read-only review did not rerun the Windows scenario.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This protected, broad, compatibility-sensitive PR needs maintainer review and final-head proof rather than an automated cleanup or repair PR.

target.title: fix(cli): harden Windows node pairing and approvals
target.body:
## Summary
- preserve stable Windows node/device identity across reconnects and same-key metadata refresh approvals, while still rotating unsafe/stale/revoked/mismatched device tokens
- harden node pairing approval authz, pending/paired node presentation, and gateway node-event routing so Windows nodes are less likely to fall back to manual/TUI repair paths
- route devices/nodes/gateway CLI RPC helpers through direct local gateway-client auth for explicit loopback shared token/password calls, without letting stale inactive env/config tokens suppress device-token fallback
- make host-native Windows exec approval get/set payloads work through the dedicated `exec.approvals.node.*` gateway methods, including native payloads that omit `payloadJSON`
- block raw `node.invoke systemExecApprovals.*` / `system.execApprovals.*` policy mutation paths and keep exec policy mutation on audited dedicated methods
- render host-native Windows node exec approval snapshots read-only in Control UI, including normalization for malformed node-supplied rules
- clarify the `nodes invoke system.run` CLI error so users are pointed to agent `/exec host=node`, not a nonexistent `openclaw exec` command
- make build/package TypeScript helper scripts run through the repo `tsx` loader so Node builds without built-in TypeScript support can complete

## Draft Snapshot
Current pushed draft head: `c4d3d613e9f6ea9c55730123c2bca2057d43dff9`.

This is pushed for review now. It remains draft because the broad changed gate is still blocked by existing repo-wide oxlint debt, not by touched files in this branch.

## Proof On This Snapshot
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Touched build/mock lint passed: `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json scripts/build-all.mjs test/scripts/build-all.test.ts src/cli/program.test-mocks.ts`.
- Focused build/test-mock regression passed: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/program.nodes-basic.e2e.test.ts test/scripts/build-all.test.ts` passed 2 shards / 44 tests.
- Exact helper that failed in pond passed locally through `tsx`: `node --import tsx scripts/write-plugin-sdk-entry-dts.ts`.
- Focused CLI regression previously passed on this branch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/nodes-cli.coverage.test.ts` passed 1 shard / 19 tests.
- Focused UI/controller regression previously passed on this branch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/exec-approvals.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/nodes.devices.test.ts` passed 1 shard / 7 tests.
- Wider Windows-node/pairing/approval regression set previously passed on this branch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/call.test.ts src/cli/direct-loopback-gateway-auth.test.ts src/cli/gateway-rpc.runtime.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/call.test.ts src/cli/devices-cli.runtime.timeout.test.ts src/cli/devices-cli.test.ts src/cli/nodes-cli.coverage.test.ts src/cli/program.nodes-basic.e2e.test.ts src/gateway/server.node-invoke-approval-bypass.test.ts src/gateway/node-registry.test.ts src/gateway/node-command-policy.test.ts src/gateway/node-connect-reconcile.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/nodes.invoke-wake.test.ts src/infra/device-pairing.test.ts src/infra/node-pairing.test.ts src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/exec-approvals.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/nodes.devices.test.ts` passed 7 Vitest shards / 408 tests.
- Autoreview local rerun clean after the build fix: `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`.
- Autoreview local rerun clean after the shared program mock fix: `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`.
- Current-head focused regression passed after the upstream force-update: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/nodes-cli.coverage.test.ts src/cli/exec-approvals-cli.test.ts src/gateway/server.node-invoke-approval-bypass.test.ts packages/gateway-protocol/src/exec-approvals-validators.test.ts` passed 3 Vitest shards / 48 tests.
- Current-head autoreview rerun clean after the upstream force-update: `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` reported `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.

## Windows Node Pond Proof
- Signed prerelease asset used: `OpenClawCompanion-Setup-x64.exe`, release `v0.6.0-alpha.14`, digest `sha256:36405bd72e2c11bae0aa051b551b339982a66ffb59820d977ad9983dbf0b4d98`.
- Linux gateway checkout refreshed to exact current draft head `c4d3d613e9f6ea9c55730123c2bca2057d43dff9`; full `CI=true pnpm build` passed under Node `v22.22.1` / pnpm `11.2.2`, including `tsdown`, `build:plugin-sdk:dts`, `write-plugin-sdk-entry-dts`, `ui:build`, `write-cli-startup-metadata`, and `write-cli-compat`.
- Runtime reported `OpenClaw 2026.5.30 (c4d3d61)` and `/health` returned live after gateway restart on port `18790`. The current CLI rejected the old `--bind custom` start path without `gateway.customBindHost`; restart succeeded with the supported `--bind lan` path.
- Signed binary verification on both Windows boxes passed: installed `OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI.exe` under the per-user `OpenClawTray` app dir, product version `0.6.0-alpha.14+Branch.master.Sha.eb06fba21c44b5d2220e83992b8da59bfcc1e67a...`, file version `0.6.0.0`, Authenticode status `Valid`, signer `OpenClaw Foundation`.
- Pond pairing proof at exact refreshed gateway runtime passed: 2 connected paired Windows nodes, 0 pending pair requests, staleKnownNodeCount=0, and each connected node reported caps=7 and commands=20.
- Feature smoke at exact refreshed gateway runtime passed on a Windows node: `device.info`, `device.status`, `camera.list`, and `screen.snapshot` with a non-empty 640x480 PNG payload.
- Per-node command proof passed: `system.which` resolved `cmd` and `powershell` on both connected Windows nodes.
- Exec approval smoke at exact refreshed gateway runtime passed: `exec.approvals.node.get` returned enabled=true/default=deny/rules=22 on both nodes; raw `node.invoke system.execApprovals.get` was rejected; a temporary native allow rule was set via `exec.approvals.node.set`; `system.run` returned exit=0; policy restore succeeded; the same command was denied after restore.
- Post-restart pairing/reconnect proof passed: after restarting the active exact-head Linux gateway runtime, `node.list` returned the same 2 connected paired Windows nodes, each still reporting caps=7 and commands=20 with `system.run`, `system.which`, and native exec-approval get/set commands present.

- Follow-up pond config drift check passed: stale `%APPDATA%\OpenClawTray` files on the two Windows boxes were not the live node configs. The desktop task launches with `OPENCLAW_TRAY_DATA_DIR` under `AppData\Local\Temp` and that live data dir points at `ws://10.42.1.7:18790`; the clean-box scheduled task runs as `openclawpond`, whose active `settings.json` / `gateways.json` also point at `ws://10.42.1.7:18790`.
- Current-head post-restart pairing proof passed on `c4d3d61`: `nodes list --connected --json` returned 2 connected Windows nodes; both reported platform=`windows`, commands=20, caps=7, and advertised `system.run`, `system.which`, `system.execApprovals.get`, and `system.execApprovals.set`.
- Current-head exec approval proof passed on `c4d3d61`: `approvals get --node` returned enabled=true/default=deny/rules=22 with host-native policy notes; raw `gateway call node.invoke` for `system.execApprovals.get` was blocked with `use exec.approvals.node.*`; `system.run` of the base-allowed `hostname` command returned exit=0 on both Windows nodes (`cbxcbx2a25b4920`, `cbxcbx58d8b308d`).
- Post-restart exec approval round trip passed on both Windows nodes: `exec.approvals.node.get` returned default=deny/rules=22, raw `node.invoke system.execApprovals.get` stayed blocked, a temporary native allow rule was observed through get/set, `system.run` crossed only while that rule was present, restore returned rule count to 22, and the same run was denied after restore.
- New CLI error copy was verified on the refreshed remote build: `nodes invoke --command system.run` exits 1 with `use an agent /exec host=node request instead of nodes invoke`.

## Known Draft Items
- `GOMAXPROCS=2 CI=true OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 pnpm check:changed` still fails in `lint core` on unrelated repo-wide `typescript/no-unsafe-type-assertion` backlog. The run reached `src/acp`, `src/agents`, and `src/auto-reply` ambient failures before being stopped; touched-file lint for this branch is clean.
target.author: vincentkoc
